definition: The Kalecki-Levy identity mechanically channels fiscal deficits (currently 6-7% of GDP) to corporate profits, simultaneously sustaining interest coverage ratios (supporting credit quality) and the P/E denominator (supporting equity valuations). The system's current resilience is fiscally contingent, not structurally earned. Fiscal consolidation exceeding 2pp GDP would trigger credit cycle downshift within 4-6 quarters.
evidence: Estimated 100-150bp of spread suppression, though residual-based attribution with wide uncertainty (50-200bp)
Godley's 1999 Levy Institute prediction as out-of-sample validation
Independent derivation by generalist_01 and macro_strategist_02 of $20-40/share estimate
Reflexive loop: deficits sustain earnings sustain valuations sustain wealth effect sustain consumption
Kalecki profit equation links fiscal deficit to corporate profit support
Deficit-to-EPS transmission observable in national accounts data
Provides structural explanation for MOVE-VIX divergence
Identified as key mechanism by six of eight agents
open_questions: What is the precise magnitude of the fiscal offset to monetary tightening?
How sustainable is this channel given the fiscal trajectory?
Political probability and timeline for fiscal consolidation
Whether reflexive loop can persist without accelerating deficit growth
Is the channel as potent when deficit is interest-expense-driven (~$1T to bondholders with low MPCs) vs spending-driven?
Does fiscal deficit composition matter for the VIX-suppressing channel?
Could deficit narrowing invert the divergence (VIX up, MOVE down)?
